content.full_text:
June 20, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
Attention: Scott Hallabrin
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request for Advice

Gentlemen:

I am an incumbent member of the Long Beach City Council having been most recently elected in 1988 to a four-year term. During that campaign, my controlled committee was "Tom Clark for City Council", and that committee is still in existence.

This year, I filed and campaigned for the separately elected office of Mayor with a controlled committee of "Clark for Mayor". The campaign was unsuccessful and concluded with a deficit that I am now in the process of repaying.

May I lawfully transfer funds solicited either in the past or in the future under the auspices of the "Tom Clark for City Council" controlled committee to the "Clark for Mayor Committee" for the purpose of retiring the mayoral campaign deficit?

In this regard, I call your attention to Advice Letter, File No. I-90-202 summarized in the FPPC Bulletin of June 1990 (Vol. 16, No. 6).

Thank you for your expeditious response to this inquiry. If you wish additional information, you can contact me at (213) 590-6918.

Very truly yours,

TOM CLARK
COUNCILMAN, 4TH DISTRICT
